binaries.title: msisadrv.sys - MSI System Agent Driver - no attacker-reachable security change; KMDF static-stub rebuild (init-time bounds-check hardening) plus stack-init/codegen refactors

changed_functions.name: memset
key_changes: Code-generation difference in the inlined memset/bulk-zero implementation

changed_functions.name: FxStubBindClasses
key_changes: Refactored to call new shared helper FxGetNextClassBindInfo
Added zero-entry skipping via the helper
Added DbgPrintEx telemetry on bind failure and on invalid image
Structural reorganization of the class-bind iteration
changed_functions.security_notes: Init-time KMDF static-stub loader code. The UNPATCHED FxStubBindClasses already performs BOTH a start check (entry+4 > __KMDF_CLASS_BIND_END at 0x1C0001223) AND a full-entry check (entry+0x50 > __KMDF_CLASS_BIND_END at 0x1C0001232) before reading the callback pointer at entry+0x38. The patched change is a structural refactor into FxGetNextClassBindInfo plus zero-entry skipping and DbgPrintEx telemetry, not a newly added bounds check. It iterates a compile-time, linker-bracketed table inside the driver image and is not reachable from any untrusted surface.

changed_functions.name: FxStubUnbindClasses
key_changes: Refactored to call new shared helper FxGetNextClassBindInfo
Added DbgPrintEx telemetry
Structural reorganization of the class-unbind iteration
changed_functions.security_notes: Teardown-time KMDF static-stub code, mirror of FxStubBindClasses. Iterates the same compile-time linker-bracketed table; not reachable from an untrusted surface.

changed_functions.name: FxStubInitTypes
key_changes: Added full-entry bounds check before field access: lea rax,[rcx+0x28]; cmp rax,rdi; ja fail (0x1C00014E9-0x1C00014F0)
Added zero-entry skipping loop over empty 8-byte slots
Added NULL pointer check (test rcx,rcx; jz fail)
Changed fixed 0x28 stride to variable stride (i += *i)
Changed the invalid-image return path and added DbgPrintEx telemetry
changed_functions.security_notes: Init-time KMDF static-stub loader code (FxStubInitTypes, decompiler symbol confirmed at 0x1C0001334 unpatched / 0x1C000147C patched). The UNPATCHED loop checks only the START of each 0x28-byte object-type-init entry against the __KMDF_TYPE_INIT_END marker (cmp rbx,rdi at 0x1C000137D), then reads a callback pointer from entry+0x20 (0x1C0001366), calls it via the CFG dispatch, and writes the result to entry+0x18. It does not verify that the full 0x28-byte entry fits below the end marker. The PATCHED build adds that full-entry check plus a zero-entry skip and NULL check. This is a genuine OOB-read hardening (CWE-125), but the iterated table is compile-time KMDF metadata delimited by linker markers inside the driver image; there is no attacker-controlled input, and the code runs only at driver load. Not reachable from an untrusted surface. See security_findings entry.

changed_functions.name: FxGetNextClassBindInfo
key_changes: New helper split out of FxStubBindClasses/FxStubUnbindClasses
Skips empty 8-byte slots and validates that a class-bind entry (0x50 bytes) fully fits within the table
changed_functions.security_notes: New shared bounds-validation helper introduced by the KMDF static-stub rebuild. Init/teardown-time only, not reachable from an untrusted surface.

changed_functions.name: DriverEntry
key_changes: Stack-frame setup and register-allocation differences; zero-initialization via xorps/zero register
Import/data offset relocations

changed_functions.name: MsIsaEvtDeviceAdd
key_changes: More thorough zero-initialization of the WDF I/O queue config and interface-descriptor stack structures using a zero register
Register-allocation differences
Import/data offset relocations
changed_functions.security_notes: Device-add (init) callback. Defense-in-depth stack hygiene; no behavioral security change and not an untrusted surface.

changed_functions.name: FxDriverEntryWorker
key_changes: Added DbgPrintEx error logging on the DriverEntry failure path
Restructured the branch (js -> jns) to accommodate the new debug block
Import/data offset relocations

changed_functions.name: MsIsaEvtDeviceProcessQueryInterface
key_changes: Zero-initializes the ResultLength stack slot ([rsp+0x44]) before the device-property query — uninitialized in unpatched
Reorders which named stack slot serves as buffer vs. ResultLength
Introduces a zero register (esi=0) for the initializations
changed_functions.security_notes: WDF query-interface callback for GUID_TRANSLATOR_INTERFACE_STANDARD (NOT an IOCTL/EvtIoDeviceControl handler). The ResultLength parameter is output-only and its value is never read back by this function; the forwarded property buffer slot is zero-initialized in BOTH builds. Therefore no information disclosure. Defense-in-depth stack zero-init plus codegen churn. See security_findings entry.

changed_functions.name: MsIsaPnPIrpPreProcessingCallback
key_changes: Reordered the ISA-PnP bus-info structure field writes and the IoStatus.Status assignment
Register-allocation differences
Import/data offset relocations
changed_functions.security_notes: Net field values written to the allocated bus-info structure are equivalent between builds. Cosmetic.

security_findings.vulnerability_class: Out-of-bounds read of a callback pointer / missing full-entry bounds check (CWE-125)
security_findings.description: The unpatched FxStubInitTypes iterates a table of 0x28-byte object-type-init records delimited by the linker markers __KMDF_TYPE_INIT_START/__KMDF_TYPE_INIT_END. The loop continuation test compares only the START of the current entry against the end marker (cmp rbx,rdi at 0x1C000137D); when the entry's size field equals 0x28 it reads a callback pointer from entry+0x20 (0x1C0001366), calls it through the Control Flow Guard dispatch (0x1C000136F), and stores the result at entry+0x18 (0x1C0001375). It does not verify the full 0x28-byte entry fits below the end marker before those accesses. The patched build (0x1C000147C) adds a full-entry check (lea rax,[rcx+0x28]; cmp rax,rdi; ja fail at 0x1C00014E9-0x1C00014F0), a zero-entry skip loop, and a NULL check, and emits DbgPrintEx on failure. This is a genuine OOB-read hardening, but the iterated table is compile-time KMDF metadata bracketed by linker markers inside the driver's own image, walked only during driver load. There is no attacker-controlled input on this path; the sibling stub FxStubBindClasses already performed a full-entry check in the unpatched build. Not reachable from an untrusted surface.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: Runs automatically during driver load (FxDriverEntry -> FxDriverEntryWorker -> FxStubInitTypes). The table is fixed compile-time metadata generated by the KMDF headers; under a normally built image the loop terminates cleanly at the end marker. The missing full-entry check could only matter for a malformed driver image, which the toolchain does not emit.
security_findings.primitive: None reachable. No attacker input on this path; no observed runtime memory-corruption or control-flow primitive.
security_findings.assembly:
; UNPATCHED FxStubInitTypes @ 0x1C0001334
0x1C000133E  lea     rax, __KMDF_TYPE_INIT_START
0x1C0001345  lea     rdi, __KMDF_TYPE_INIT_END
0x1C000134C  cmp     rax, rdi
0x1C000134F  jbe     0x1C0001358
0x1C0001358  lea     rbx, __KMDF_TYPE_INIT_END
0x1C000135F  jmp     0x1C000137D
0x1C0001361  cmp     dword [rbx], 0x28
0x1C0001364  jnz     0x1C000138F
0x1C0001366  mov     rax, [rbx+0x20]                ; read callback pointer (no full-entry check)
0x1C000136A  test    rax, rax
0x1C000136D  jz      0x1C0001379
0x1C000136F  call    cs:__guard_dispatch_icall_fptr ; CFG-guarded indirect call
0x1C0001375  mov     [rbx+0x18], rax
0x1C0001379  add     rbx, 0x28
0x1C000137D  cmp     rbx, rdi                        ; only checks entry START vs end
0x1C0001380  jb      0x1C0001361
0x1C0001382  xor     eax, eax

; PATCHED FxStubInitTypes @ 0x1C000147C (added checks)
0x1C00014E9  lea     rax, [rcx+0x28]                 ; full-entry extent
0x1C00014ED  cmp     rax, rdi
0x1C00014F0  ja      0x1C0001519                     ; entry must fully fit -> fail
0x1C00014F2  cmp     dword [rcx], 0x28
0x1C00014FA  test    rcx, rcx
0x1C00014FD  jz      0x1C0001519                     ; NULL check
0x1C00014FF  mov     rax, [rcx+0x20]
0x1C0001508  call    cs:__guard_dispatch_icall_fptr

breakpoints.why: Break at function entry during driver load. rax/rdi hold __KMDF_TYPE_INIT_START/__KMDF_TYPE_INIT_END; watch rbx advance by 0x28. The unpatched check at 0x1C000137D validates only the entry start.

key_offsets: 0x1C000137D: cmp rbx, rdi — start-only bounds check (patched adds entry+0x28 check)
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: Runs automatically at driver load. The table is compile-time KMDF metadata; under a normally built image the loop terminates at the end marker. Not reachable from user mode.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: Under a normally built image the loop reads valid entries and stops at __KMDF_TYPE_INIT_END. There is no attacker-controlled path to place a malformed truncated final entry.
debugging_aids.notes: The table between __KMDF_TYPE_INIT_START and __KMDF_TYPE_INIT_END holds KMDF object-type initialization records generated by the WDF headers at compile time. This is statically-linked KMDF loader-stub code shared across KMDF drivers of this build; the change is defensive hardening, not a fix for an attacker-reachable condition in msisadrv.

call_chain: In-kernel PnP interface query for GUID_TRANSLATOR_INTERFACE_STANDARD
security_findings.description: MsIsaEvtDeviceProcessQueryInterface is the driver's WDF query-interface callback for GUID_TRANSLATOR_INTERFACE_STANDARD (registered via the interface descriptor built in MsIsaEvtDeviceAdd). It is NOT an IOCTL/EvtIoDeviceControl handler and is not reachable via DeviceIoControl. It validates the interface parameter (a4==2), size (*a3>=0x30), and the requested GUID, queries two device properties (IDs 0xd and 0xe) via WdfFunctions_01015+0x288, then calls HalGetInterruptTranslator (HalDispatchTable+0x78). In the unpatched build the stack slot [rsp+0x44] used as the property query's output ResultLength is not zeroed before the first call; the patched build zeroes it (mov [rsp+0x44], esi with esi=0) and swaps which named slot is buffer vs. ResultLength. Because ResultLength is output-only and its value is never read back by this function, and the forwarded property buffer slot is zero-initialized in BOTH builds, there is no information disclosure. Defense-in-depth stack zero-init bundled with register-allocation/codegen differences.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: Reached only when an in-kernel component queries the device for GUID_TRANSLATOR_INTERFACE_STANDARD during PnP interface negotiation. Not reachable from user mode.
security_findings.primitive: None. ResultLength is output-only and never consumed; the property buffer is zero-initialized in both builds; HalGetInterruptTranslator is invoked identically in both builds.
security_findings.assembly:
; UNPATCHED @ 0x1C00062A0 (first property query, ID 0xd)
0x1C00062E9  mov     [rsp+0x40], eax                ; buffer slot = GUID-compare residual (0)
0x1C00062ED  lea     rcx, [rsp+0x44]                ; &ResultLength slot (not initialized)
0x1C00062FF  mov     [rsp+0x28], rcx                ; arg6 = ResultLength = &[rsp+0x44]
0x1C0006307  lea     rcx, [rsp+0x40]                ; &buffer slot
0x1C000630C  mov     [rsp+0x20], rcx                ; arg5 = Buffer = &[rsp+0x40]
0x1C0006311  mov     rax, [rax+0x288]               ; WdfFunctions_01015+0x288 (device-property query)
0x1C0006318  lea     r8d, [r9+9]                    ; PropertyId = 0xd
0x1C0006323  call    cs:__guard_dispatch_icall_fptr
0x1C00063A0  mov     rax, [rax+0x78]                ; HalDispatchTable+0x78 = HalGetInterruptTranslator
0x1C00063AE  mov     ecx, [rsp+0x40]                ; forward property buffer to HAL call
0x1C00063B2  call    cs:__guard_dispatch_icall_fptr

; PATCHED @ 0x1C00062B0
0x1C00062BF  xor     esi, esi
0x1C00062C4  mov     [rsp+0x40], esi                ; slot zeroed
0x1C0006311  lea     rcx, [rsp+0x40]
0x1C0006316  mov     [rsp+0x28], rcx                ; arg6 = ResultLength = &[rsp+0x40]
0x1C0006321  lea     rcx, [rsp+0x44]
0x1C0006326  mov     [rsp+0x44], esi                ; NEW: slot zeroed
0x1C000632A  mov     [rsp+0x20], rcx                ; arg5 = Buffer = &[rsp+0x44]
0x1C0006344  call    cs:__guard_dispatch_icall_fptr
0x1C00063CB  mov     ecx, [rsp+0x44]                ; forward property buffer to HAL call

breakpoints.why: Break at the query-interface callback entry. r9d must be 2 and word [r8] must be >= 0x30 to pass the guards; [rdx]/[rdx+8] must equal GUID_TRANSLATOR_INTERFACE_STANDARD. Observe [rsp+0x44] before the first property query (uninitialized in unpatched, zeroed in patched).

key_offsets: 0x1C00062ED: lea rcx, [rsp+0x44] — ResultLength slot (uninitialized in unpatched)
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: Invoked by the kernel PnP manager / a peer driver querying GUID_TRANSLATOR_INTERFACE_STANDARD; not reachable from a user-mode handle or DeviceIoControl.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: In the unpatched build [rsp+0x44] holds prior stack residue before the first property query, but the value is used only as an output ResultLength and is never read back, so it does not leak. The patched build zeroes it.
debugging_aids.notes: HalDispatchTable+0x78 resolves to HalGetInterruptTranslator, consistent with the translator interface. There is no PCI-configuration-space read/write primitive here. The requested-interface GUID constants decode to GUID_TRANSLATOR_INTERFACE_STANDARD {6C154A92-AACF-11D0-8D2A-00A0C906B244}.

summary: The diff between msisadrv_unpatched.sys and msisadrv_patched.sys is a KMDF (Kernel-Mode Driver Framework) static-stub library rebuild plus code-generation refactors. The linked-in WDF loader stubs (FxStubBindClasses, FxStubUnbindClasses, FxStubInitTypes, and a new shared helper FxGetNextClassBindInfo) gained tighter bounds validation and DbgPrintEx telemetry. FxStubInitTypes (0x1C0001334 -> 0x1C000147C) is the only stub that genuinely adds a full-entry bounds check (entry+0x28 vs the end marker) before reading a callback pointer at entry+0x20; FxStubBindClasses already performed both start and full-entry checks in the unpatched build. All of this stub code runs only at driver load/teardown over a compile-time, linker-bracketed metadata table inside the driver image and is not reachable from any untrusted surface. MsIsaEvtDeviceProcessQueryInterface (0x1C00062A0 -> 0x1C00062B0) is a WDF query-interface callback (not an IOCTL handler) whose only change is zero-initializing an output-only ResultLength stack slot and codegen churn; the value is never read back, so there is no information disclosure. No attacker-reachable vulnerability is fixed or introduced by this patch.
